channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Jewish and Muslim People Face a Wave of Hate. Can Schools Turn the Tide?
item.description: New York City is rolling out lessons on Jewish and Muslim American history. Too often, some leaders say, what students know comes from TikTok and social media.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Afsa Irfan, a seventh grader, participated in a discussion about enslaved people during a class at the Urban Assembly School for Leadership and Empowerment in Brooklyn.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 23, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: London Arson Attack on Jewish Ambulance Service Investigated as Hate Crime
item.description: The Metropolitan Police said on Monday that it was investigating the episode as an antisemitic hate crime.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:description: Emergency personnel at the scene where the police say four ambulances were set on fire, in North London on Monday.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Power Outages Are Reported in Tehran as Israel Launches Strikes
item.description: Residents reported widespread power outages across the capital. Israel’s military said it had begun a new wave of airstrikes targeting infrastructure in the city, but did not provide further details.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: acclimate
item.description: This word has appeared in 154 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Flights Grounded at LaGuardia Airport as New York Fire Department Responds to Incident
item.description: A ground stop was in effect early Monday as the Fire Department said it was responding to a runway incident. A New York Times journalist saw an Air Canada plane on the runway with a sheared-off nose.
item.dc:creator: Dakota Santiago, Max Kim and Mike Ives

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times

item.title: Stocks Tumble as Oil Prices Rise
item.description: Investors braced for a fourth week of market turmoil caused by the war in the Middle East
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Mette Frederiksen Changed Denmark
item.description: Whatever Mette Frederiksen does next, Denmark has fundamentally changed.
item.dc:creator: Martin Krasnik

media:content.media:credit: Thomas Traasdahl/Ritzau Scanpix Denmark, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen of Denmark has called a snap election for March 24.

item.title: ‘Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen,’ Plus 6 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new horror thriller premieres and Miley Cyrus celebrates the 20th anniversary of “Hannah Montana.”
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Adam DiMarco and Camila Morrone in “Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen.”

item.title: Caught Between Two Conflicts, Afghans Flee Iran
item.description: The war in Iran is choking off Afghanistan’s main economic lifeline and has forced at least 70,000 Afghan workers and students there back home to a nation embroiled in another conflict.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Kiana Hayeri
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: People crossing the border from Iran to Afghanistan last week at Islam Qala.

item.title: In Denmark’s Election, How Will the Woman Who Took On Trump Fare?
item.description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is the biggest force this country has seen in decades. The crisis in Greenland has energized her, but are voters itching for change?
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen greeting constituents during a campaign event on Tuesday in her hometown Aalborg, Denmark.

item.title: No Corrections: March 23, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, March 23, 2026.

item.title: How Obama’s Strategist Discouraged Biden From Running in 2016
item.description: In a new set of oral histories, David Plouffe, President Barack Obama’s political adviser, described how he urged Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. not to challenge Hillary Clinton for the nomination.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:description: David Plouffe, President Barack Obama’s political strategist, in 2011. He publicly defended his advice to Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. in 2015 not to run for president the following year.

item.title: Quote of the Day: War Descends On Shorefront In Posh Beirut
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, March 23, 2026.

item.title: Suspect in Chicago Student’s Killing Was in U.S. Illegally, D.H.S. Says
item.description: Sheridan Gorman, 18, was killed last week near Loyola University Chicago. The Trump administration has sought to highlight crimes committed by undocumented people in its deportation campaign.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

item.title: U.N. Climate Report Finds Growing Energy Imbalance on Earth
item.description: The continued burning of fossil fuels is locking heat in Earth’s atmosphere, oceans and land — instead of allowing it to reflect back into space, a new report finds.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler

media:content.media:description: Greenhouse gases released by humanity have reached their highest level in at least 800,000 years, diminishing the reflectivity of the Earth and its ability to send energy from the sun back into space.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3 Premiere Recap: The Old Razzle-Dazzle
item.description: The HBO comedy returned for a new season on Sunday, more than 11 years after the previous one.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:description: Lisa Kudrow is back for a new season of “The Comeback” on HBO.

item.title: Iranians Fear Trump’s Threat to Strike Power Plants
item.description: With President Trump threatening to strike Iranian power plants, some war-weary civilians are panicking over a possible new debacle.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran residents on Sunday, when tensions were rising over whether power would be cut to the capital and home to 10 million people.

item.title: Share Your Thoughts on Our Media Literacy Newsletter
item.description: Are you an educator interested in adding journalism lessons to your curriculum? We want to hear from you.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Kim

item.title: Jeff Webb, Who Built a Competitive Cheerleading Empire, Dies at 76
item.description: Through Varsity Spirit, the company he established in 1974, he turned cheerleading into a multibillion-dollar juggernaut and exerted control over almost every aspect of it.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: Jeff Webb in 2011. Competitive cheering as it exists in today, with high school and college students performing routines set to music, is largely the product of innovations he brought about.

item.title: Pro-Palestinian Activists Rally in Support of Freed Columbia Protester
item.description: After speaking alongside city officials in Paterson, N.J., Leqaa Kordia, who was recently released after a year in ICE detention, led a crowd in chants of “globalize the intifada.”
item.dc:creator: Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Michelle V. Agins/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Leqaa Kordia greeted supporters outside City Hall in Paterson, N.J., days after her release from federal immigration detention, where she had been held for a year.

item.title: At NYC Airports, Long TSA Lines and Frustrated Travelers
item.description: The shortage of checkpoint workers created lines of at least three hours at LaGuardia Airport. Many passengers doubted ICE agents were the solution.
item.dc:creator: Tara Terranova and Ellen Yan

media:content.media:credit: Tara Terranova for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Passengers waited for hours in the security line at New York's LaGuardia Airport on Sunday.

item.title: Irán lanza misiles contra la base militar en Diego García, pero EE. UU. sigue fuera de su alcance
item.description: El intento de ataque iraní contra una base militar conjunta del Reino Unido y EE. UU. a 4000 kilómetros de distancia plantea interrogantes sobre el alcance de su arsenal.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: Center for Strategic and International Studies

media:content.media:description: La isla Diego García, sede de una base militar conjunta del Reino Unido y Estados Unidos, en una foto distribuida por la Marina estadounidense.

item.title: Una estatua de Cristóbal Colón vuelve a instalarse cerca de la Casa Blanca
item.description: La réplica de un monumento, que fue derribado por un grupo de manifestantes en 2020, fue colocada frente al edificio Eisenhower Executive Office en Washington.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una estatua de Cristóbal Colón fue instalada el domingo por la noche frente al edificio Eisenhower Executive Office en Washington.

item.title: How the G.O.P. and Democrats Are Talking About the Surge in Gas Prices
item.description: Republicans have largely been on defense against Democratic criticism. Here are five arguments advanced by figures in both parties.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Me alegro’: Trump reacciona a la muerte del exfiscal que investigó su campaña de 2016
item.description: Robert Mueller fue director del FBI y en 2017 investigó los esfuerzos de Rusia por inmiscuirse en las elecciones presidenciales del año anterior e inclinar el resultado a favor de Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Robert Mueller compareció ante el Comité de Inteligencia de la Cámara de Representantes en 2019.

item.title: Oahu Residents Assess Flood Damage as Rain Drenches Maui
item.description: Hawaii has been pounded for over a week by back-to-back storms. Weather officials said that 10 to 14 inches of rain fell on Thursday into Friday on Oahu, unleashing severe flooding. The storm has moved to Maui, where flash flood warnings were issued on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: A flooded neighborhood in Waialua, Hawaii, on Friday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 23, 2026
item.description: For puzzlers of all generations, Matthew Luter hits the sweet spot.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott

media:content.media:description: Not to be cheesy, but we really want to help you solve this puzzle.

item.title: Las nuevas drogas mortales no llegan en pastillas ni en agujas, sino en papel
item.description: Drogas fabricadas en laboratorio e impregnadas en las páginas de cartas, libros e incluso documentos legales se introducen clandestinamente a las prisiones, matan a reclusos y frustran a los investigadores.
item.dc:creator: Azam Ahmed, Matt Richtel and Meridith Kohut

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Division 9 Maximum Security Dormitory of the Cook County Department of Corrections — where inmates have been found using deadly illicit drugs — saturated onto paper — in the pages of books, letters from family and legal documents.

item.title: Heat Wave in California and Other Western States Wilts a More Air-Conditioned U.S.
item.description: In recent years, more homes have added air-conditioning, something that may come in handy this week as some areas are expected to see temperatures 40 degrees above normal.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder and Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: San Francisco residents at Baker Beach during last week’s heat wave.

item.title: For ‘Buffy’ Fans, Nicholas Brendon’s Xander Was a Complicated Everyman
item.description: The actor, who died on Friday, was a fan favorite on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” even as his character mixed quiet heroism with hostility toward the show’s women.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:description: Nicholas Brendon, bottom left, as Xander Harris in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.” His castmates included Alyson Hannigan, top left, Charisma Carpenter, right, and Sarah Michelle Gellar, center.

item.title: Trump’s Ultimatum to Iran
item.description: The president’s messages on the war have shifted, but the latest threat might be a sign that he’s moving to a harder stance.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Israel’s Missile Defense Under Scrutiny After Iranian Attack
item.description: Two missiles landed hours apart, wreaking havoc in two towns near a heavily guarded nuclear site in the Negev Desert.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Peering Sunday at damage where a missile struck the night before in Arad, Israel.

item.title: French Far Right Falls Short of Statement Win in Yardstick Local Races
item.description: France’s far right hoped for major gains in Sunday’s municipal elections, a key bellwether moment before a presidential election next year. Its results were mixed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Catherine Porter

media:content.media:description: Casting a ballot on Sunday in Lyon, southeastern France.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli military said its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon would intensify, while Iran threatened to attack civilian infrastructure if President Trump followed through with an ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Trump dice que enviará agentes del ICE a los aeropuertos el lunes
item.description: El presidente lanzó la amenaza de desplegar agentes del ICE en un aparente intento de obligar a los demócratas a aprobar un nuevo presupuesto para el Departamento de Seguridad Nacional.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Michael Gold

item.title: Meteorite Crashes Through Roof of House Near Houston
item.description: No one was injured, but a woman was startled on Saturday when a meteorite pierced the roof of her home, ricocheted off the floor and struck a bedroom ceiling.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:description: Chief Fred C. Windisch of the Ponderosa Fire Department in Harris County, Texas, shared a photo of an apparent meteorite that crashed through the roof of a two-story house on Saturday.

item.title: ICE Agents Will Be Deployed to U.S. Airports, White House Confirms
item.description: Tom Homan, the White House border czar, confirmed on Sunday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents would help security officials ease long lines at airports starting Monday. Transportation Security Administration officers have been working without pay amid a partial government shutdown that has led some workers to call out of work or quit.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Security lines at the John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York on Sunday. Tom Homan, the White House border czar, said ICE agents would be deployed to help with the waits.

item.title: J. Michael Bishop, Nobel Prize Winner for Cancer Research, Dies at 90
item.description: He helped discover cancer-causing genes. Later, as chancellor of the University of California, San Francisco, he led a major expansion.
item.dc:creator: Delthia Ricks

media:content.media:description: J. Michael Bishop in his lab in 1982. He won a Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1989 and was the chancellor of the University of California, San Francisco, from 1998 to 2009.

item.title: Christopher Columbus Statue Is Installed on White House Grounds
item.description: The statue of the explorer, a replica of one that protesters toppled in 2020, was placed outside the Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A statue of Christopher Columbus was installed overnight in front of the Eisenhower Executive Office Building in Washington on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Thought It Could Spur Rebellion Inside Iran. That Hasn’t Happened.
item.description: President Trump’s hopes that an Israeli plan to ignite an internal uprising against Iran’s theocratic government could bring the war to a swift end have so far been dashed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Edward Wong and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The aftermath of U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Saturday. Three weeks into the war, American and Israeli intelligence assessments have concluded that the theocratic Iranian government is weakened but intact.

item.title: A Window Into the Lives of New York City Renters
item.description: For Sunday’s issue of The New York Times Magazine, Gail Albert Halaban photographed city dwellers inside their apartments from across the street — with their permission, of course.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Gail Albert Halaban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Residents of Bedford-Stuyvesant in Brooklyn, photographed for a recent article in The New York Times Magazine.

item.title: Attack on a Sudan Hospital Kills Dozens, Head of W.H.O. Says
item.description: The group’s director general said 13 children were among those killed in the latest violence in the Darfur region, and he decried the targeting of health care facilities in the civil war.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel

media:content.media:description: Refugees waiting to be registered at the Oure Cassoni camp in Chad after fleeing the civil war in Sudan, in February. The war has created a devastating humanitarian crisis.

item.title: Spring Cooking, Activate!
item.description: Lemon butter salmon, asparagus with furikake and chicken with spring vegetables, you’re up.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Ko

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Rebecca Jurkevich.
media:content.media:description: Genevieve Ko’s lemon butter salmon with dill.

item.title: ICE to Help TSA at Airports Amid Partial Shutdown, Trump’s Border Czar Says
item.description: Tom Homan, President Trump’s chief border official, cast the operation largely as one to help ease long security lines. The Homeland Security Department said 14 airports across the country would be involved.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Hamed Aleaziz and Gabe Castro-Root

media:content.media:description: Security lines at the John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York on Sunday. Tom Homan, the White House border czar, said ICE agents would be deployed to help with the waits.

item.title: Anti-Muslim Comments by G.O.P. Congressman Reflect a Rising Trend
item.description: On Capitol Hill and in Nashville, comments by Representative Andy Ogles of Tennessee have sown division and underscored a growing tolerance on the right for Islamophobia.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Emily Cochrane
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Andy Ogles, who has made a series of Islamophobic comments, represents a sizable Muslim population in a Nashville-area district.

item.title: Israel Orders Military to Intensify Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel Katz, the defense minister, said he ordered troops to destroy more bridges and buildings in southern Lebanon, stoking worries that Israel was widening a military-controlled buffer zone there.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Cars turn around on a bridge in southern Lebanon after an Israeli strike destroyed it on Sunday.

item.title: Bracing for the A.I. Economy to Come
item.description: Readers respond to two guest essays about the changes that artificial intelligence will wreak on the work force. Also: The shoes of the Trump men.

item.title: The Role of Public Theology in Crisis
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, I’m yearning for a more robust conversation about morality — in politics, in our workplaces and online.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:description: Eid al-Fitr prayer in Brooklyn on Friday.

item.title: Tehran Is Defiant After Trump Threatens Power Plants
item.description: Iran dismissed President Trump’s ultimatum to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, saying it would consider electric plants and water facilities “legitimate targets” if its electrical grid were struck.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
